docs.title: Home
docs.text:
• Business Discovery

    Search businesses using keyword, location, and industry-based discovery powered by public business data.

    {
  "query": "singapore cafe",
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "emails": []
  }
}

  • Automation Ready

    Integrate directly into internal tools, AI workflows, CRMs, and outreach automation systems.

    "nodes": [
    {
      "name": "HTTP Request",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "parameters": {
        "method": "GET",
        "url": "https://findmyclient.org/api/search"
      }
    }

  • Developer Friendly

    Simple REST API, asynchronous processing, webhook support, and self-hosted deployment options.

    {
  "endpoint": "/api/search",
  "method": "GET",
  "params": {
    "query": "singapore cafe"
  }
}

  • Built for Growth

    Designed for startups, agencies, and teams scaling lead generation efforts.

    {
  "use_case": "lead_generation",
  "scale": "startup_to_enterprise",
  "goal": "find_and_enrich_business_leads",
  "query_example": "singapore cafe"
}

How does the FindMyClient API work?

The FindMyClient API uses location-based business search powered by public web data and mapping sources to discover businesses matching your queries. It then crawls publicly available company websites and business pages to extract emails for sales, outreach, CRM, and AI workflow automation.

What kind of data can FindMyClient discover?

FindMyClient can discover publicly available business emails, websites, and location-based business data from web sources and mapping platforms. The open-source version includes a self-hosted UI that can run locally, while the API version returns structured JSON schemas optimized for AI agents, automation workflows, and developer integrations.

Can I integrate this into AI agents or workflow?

Yes. The FindMyClient API is designed for AI agents, workflow automation, CRM enrichment, and internal lead generation systems. It returns structured JSON schemas optimized for LLMs, autonomous agents, APIs, and no-code workflows such as n8n and automation pipelines.

Does FindMyClient support self-hosting?

Yes. FindMyClient supports self-hosting through its web UI, allowing developers and teams to run the platform locally or on private infrastructure for greater control, custom workflows, and internal business data operations

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Send a query, an async job runs crawling and analysis, then poll for a final JSON result with emails and business data.

docs.title: API Tokens
docs.text:
API tokens allow you to authenticate requests to the FindMyClient API.

Once signed in, users can generate and manage API tokens directly from the dashboard.

docs.title: Getting Your API Token
docs.text:
Dashboard Access

API tokens are only available to authenticated users through the FindMyClient dashboard.

  1. Sign in to your FindMyClient account.
  2. Open the Dashboard.
  3. Navigate to the API Tokens section.
  4. Generate or copy your API token.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Include your API token in the request headers when making API calls.

token: YOUR_API_TOKEN

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
    -H "token: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "query": "singapore cafe"
    }'

docs.title: Security Recommendations
docs.text:
Keep Your Token Secure

Your API token provides access to your account and API usage.

  • Keep your API token private.
  • Do not expose tokens in frontend code or public repositories.
  • Rotate tokens regularly when needed.
  • Revoke compromised tokens immediately from the dashboard.

docs.title: Built for Automation
docs.text:
FindMyClient API tokens can be used with:

  • AI agents
  • CRM enrichment systems
  • Internal business tools
  • Workflow automation platforms
  • n8n pipelines
  • Custom developer integrations

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: Latest platform and API updates for FindMyClient.

docs.title: v0.0.1 - 13 May 2026
docs.text:
Added:

Rate Limiting

- Added request rate limiting to protect the API from excessive usage.
- Implemented a global handler for HTTP `429 Too Many Requests`.
- Returns a structured JSON response when rate limits are exceeded.


Search Request Validation
Introduced a centralized validation layer for all search requests.

Input Sources Supported

- JSON request body
- Form data
- Query parameters

Validation Rules

- Query must be a string.
- Minimum length: 3 characters.
- Maximum length: 50 characters.
- Maximum word count: 8 words.

Security Improvements
Added multiple safeguards to improve request safety and prevent abuse.

- Blocks URL-based inputs (e.g. `http://`, `www.`)
- Prevents common injection patterns (SQL-like and script-based inputs)
- Detects repeated-character spam patterns
- Rejects inputs with excessive non-alphanumeric content ratio


Content Filtering
Introduced keyword-based filtering to block unsafe or irrelevant queries.

- Fraud-related terms
- Credential/password-related terms
- Gambling-related terms
- Adult content-related terms


Input Normalization

- Trims leading and trailing whitespace
- Collapses multiple spaces into a single space
- Normalizes query formatting before processing

docs.title: v0.0.2 - 24 May 2026
docs.text:
Added:

Schema Improvements (Machine Readable Results)

- Improved API response schema for consistent machine-readable output.
- Standardized response structure across all endpoints.
- Added explicit typing for key fields (e.g. query, results, metadata).
- Ensured predictable JSON keys to for agents and integrations.
- Added structured error objects with consistent error codes and messages.


API Usage Enforcement (Anti-Abuse Layer)

- Strengthened API usage controls to reduce spam and automated abuse.
- Added stricter request validation at gateway level.
- Improved rate-limit enforcement consistency across distributed requests.
- Added early request rejection to reduce backend load from invalid traffic.


Dashboard Improvements (Minor Fixes)

- Fixed UI inconsistency in dashboard data rendering.
- Improved alignment and spacing in key metric panels.
- Corrected minor display bug affecting recent request logs.
- Enhanced stability of real-time update feed under high request volume.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for the FindMyClient API.

The API is built for asynchronous search processing. Each search request is queued as a background job and returns a unique job_id. You can retrieve results by polling the result endpoint until the job status is marked as completed.

Both GET and POST methods are supported for flexible integration with applications, scripts, AI agents, and automation workflows.

Async Processing

Search requests are processed in the background to support scalable business discovery and large web data operations.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text:
The API follows a simple asynchronous workflow.

flowchart LR
    A[Submit Search Request] --> B[Receive job_id]
    B --> C[Poll Result Endpoint]
    C --> D[Status Completed]
    D --> E[Retrieve Results]

docs.title: Submit Request
docs.text:
Call the /search endpoint to create a new background search job.

POST /search

docs.title: Receive Job ID
docs.text:
The API immediately returns a unique job_id.

{
  "job_id": "654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd",
  "status": "processing"
}

docs.title: Check Status
docs.text:
Poll the result endpoint periodically to monitor progress.

GET /result/<job_id>

Example:

GET /result/654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd

docs.title: Completion
docs.text:
Once the status becomes completed, retrieve the final search results.

{
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "emails": []
  }
}

docs.title: Designed For
docs.text:
FindMyClient is designed for:

  • AI agents
  • CRM enrichment
  • Internal lead generation systems
  • Workflow automation
  • n8n pipelines
  • Business intelligence tools
  • Developer integrations

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text: The FindMyClient API has the following limitations to ensure stable, fair, and reliable usage for all users.

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
Requests are subject to rate limiting. Excessive usage may result in throttling or temporary delays.

Rate Limit Behavior

If limits are exceeded, the API will return a throttled response.

{
  "error": "Too many requests. Please wait before trying again.",
  "status": "failed"
}

docs.title: Asynchronous Processing
docs.text:
All requests are processed asynchronously.

After submitting a request, you must poll the result endpoint to retrieve the final output:

/result/{job_id}

docs.title: Processing Time
docs.text:
Processing time may vary depending on:

  • Query complexity
  • Current system load
  • Availability of external data sources

Some requests may complete quickly, while others may take longer depending on workload and crawling depth.

docs.title: Data Availability
docs.text:
Results depend on publicly available data. In some cases:

  • Business contact information may not exist
  • Email addresses may not be publicly accessible
  • Partial or incomplete results may be returned

docs.title: Concurrent Requests
docs.text:
Concurrent request volume is limited to ensure system stability.

High parallel usage may result in:

  • Request throttling
  • Increased latency
  • Delayed job processing

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
To ensure optimal performance:

  • Use controlled request intervals
  • Avoid unnecessary parallel requests
  • Cache results when possible
  • Poll results with reasonable intervals (e.g., 10–60 seconds)

docs.title: n8n Integration
docs.text:
Connect FindMyClient.org with n8n using the built-in HTTP Request node.

This integration is lightweight, flexible, and does not require a custom n8n community node. Since the FindMyClient API is REST-based, n8n can interact with it directly using standard HTTP requests.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text:
The integration follows a simple asynchronous workflow.

flowchart LR
    A[Client Request] --> B[POST /search]
    B --> C[Async Job Created]
    C --> D[Wait for Processing]
    D --> E[GET /result/{job_id}]
    E --> F[Final JSON Results]

docs.title: Creating a Search Request
docs.text: Add an HTTP Request node to your workflow.

docs.title: Request Configuration
docs.text: Setting Value Method POST URL https://findmyclient.org/api/search

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value token YOUR_API_TOKEN Content-Type application/json

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "query": "restaurants in singapore"
}

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
After submitting the request, the API returns a processing response containing a job_id.

{
  "job_id": "job_abc123",
  "status": "processing"
}

docs.title: Waiting for Processing
docs.text:
Because searches are processed asynchronously, it is recommended to add a Wait node after the initial request.

Recommended Delay

A delay between 5–10 seconds is usually sufficient for standard searches.

This reduces unnecessary polling and helps minimize API load.

docs.title: Fetching Results
docs.text:
Add a second HTTP Request node to retrieve the completed result.

Setting Value Method GET URL https://findmyclient.org/api/result/{{$json.job_id}}

Use the same authorization header from the initial request.

docs.title: Example Result
docs.text:
{
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "email": []
  }
}

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
A common production workflow looks similar to the example below.

flowchart LR
    A[Webhook Trigger]
    --> B[HTTP POST /search]
    --> C[Wait Node]
    --> D[HTTP GET /result/{job_id}]
    --> E[CRM / Spreadsheet / AI Agent]

This structure works well for:

  • CRM enrichment
  • AI prospecting systems
  • Outbound automation
  • Internal lead research workflows

docs.title: Production Recommendations
docs.text:
For larger workflows:

  • Store returned job_id values for retry handling
  • Use environment variables for API credentials
  • Add retry logic for failed requests
  • Implement rate limiting for high-volume workflows
  • Cache repeated searches where possible

docs.title: Integration Ideas
docs.text:
FindMyClient can integrate with:

  • HubSpot
  • Google Sheets
  • Airtable
  • Notion
  • Slack
  • Discord
  • Internal CRM systems

Many teams also combine FindMyClient with AI agents to automate:

  • Prospect qualification
  • Company research
  • Lead enrichment
  • Outbound workflows

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Once your integration is working, reusable n8n templates can be created for:

  • CRM enrichment
  • Automated prospect discovery
  • AI-powered lead qualification
  • Internal business workflows

This helps standardize automation pipelines and accelerate deployment across teams.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
FindMyClient.org provides a real-time API for discovering public business contact information.

A community open-source version is available for local use, while the hosted API delivers fast, scalable, up-to-date results for developers and automation workflows.

Github Repo: 

https://github.com/Rottie420/findmyclient

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://findmyclient.org/api

docs.title: GET /result/<job_id>
docs.text: Retrieve the current status and results of a search job.

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: GET /result/654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd

docs.title: Processing Response
docs.text:
Returned while the job is still running.

{
  "created_at": "Sun, 24 May 2026 00:00:46 GMT",
  "error": null,
  "result": null,
  "status": "processing",
  "user": "user@test.com"
}

docs.title: Response Fields (Processing)
docs.text: Field Type Description created_at string (timestamp) When the job was created (UTC) error string | null Execution errors, or null if none result object | null Empty until job completes status string Current job state (e.g. processing) user string (email) Account that initiated the request

docs.title: Completed Response
docs.text:
Returned once the job finishes successfully.

{
  "created_at": "Sat, 23 May 2026 23:52:26 GMT",
  "credit_cost": 60,
  "error": null,
  "result": {
    "errors": [
      {
        "code": "crawl_failed",
        "count": 8,
        "retryable": true
      }
    ],
    "input": {
      "query": "singapore cafe"
    },
    "meta": {
      "agent": "findmyclient-email-scraper-v1",
      "execution_time_seconds": 26.57,
      "timestamp": "Sat, 23 May 2026 23:52:53 GMT"
    },
    "output": {
      "emails": [
        "asxvmprobertest@gmail.com",
        "events@mercimarcelgroup.com",
        "hello@mercimarcelgroup.com",
        "press@mercimarcelgroup.com",
        "smart.journey.prober@gmail.com",
        "work@mercimarcelgroup.com"
      ],
      "total_emails_found": 6,
      "total_failed_websites": 8,
      "total_websites_scanned": 10
    },
    "status": {
      "partial_failure": true,
      "success": true
    }
  },
  "status": "completed"
}

docs.title: Job Metadata
docs.text: Field Type Description created_at string (timestamp) Job creation time (UTC) credit_cost integer API credits used error string | null Global job error (if any) status string Job state (e.g. completed)

docs.title: Result Object
docs.text: Field Type Description result object Root container for extracted data

docs.title: Errors
docs.text: Field Type Description result.errors array Non-fatal crawl errors result.errors[].code string Error identifier (e.g. crawl_failed) result.errors[].count integer Occurrence count result.errors[].retryable boolean Whether retry is possible

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Field Type Description result.input.query string Original search query

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text: Field Type Description result.meta.agent string Scraper version used result.meta.execution_time_seconds float Processing time result.meta.timestamp string Completion timestamp

docs.title: Output Data
docs.text: Field Type Description result.output.emails array Extracted email list result.output.total_emails_found integer Unique emails found result.output.total_failed_websites integer Failed website crawls result.output.total_websites_scanned integer Total scanned domains

docs.title: Status Flags
docs.text: Field Type Description result.status.partial_failure boolean Some sources failed but job completed result.status.success boolean Overall success state

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://findmyclient.org/api

docs.title: POST / GET /search
docs.text:
Submit a new search request.

AI Agent Ready

The API returns structured JSON responses optimized for AI agents, automation systems, CRMs, and developer workflows.

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text: POST /search

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "query": "singapore cafe"
}

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
  -H "token: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "singapore cafe"
  }'

docs.title: GET Request
docs.text: GET /search?query=singapore+cafe&token=YOUR-API-TOKEN

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: curl "https://findmyclient.org/api/search?query=singapore+cafe&token=YOUR-API-TOKEN"

docs.title: Search Response
docs.text:
{
  "credits_remaining": 4520,
  "job_id": "654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd",
  "status": "processing"
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text:
The following table describes the properties returned in the API response body.

Field Type Description error string Details regarding any syntax or processing errors encountered. status string CuThe current execution state of the requested job. job_id string Unique identifier assigned to the specific search job. credits_remaining int The remaining balance of API credits available to the account. user string The username or identifier associated with the active account.

docs.title: Async Processing
docs.text:
Search requests are processed asynchronously.

After submitting a search request, the API returns a job_id that can be used to track or retrieve results.

{
  "job_id": "654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd",
  "status": "processing"
}

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
This example demonstrates how to interact with the FindMyClient API using Python.

It covers the full asynchronous workflow:

  • Create a search job
  • Poll for completion
  • Retrieve final results

Info

This approach is ideal for automation scripts, backend systems, and AI agent integrations.

docs.title: Python Example
docs.text:
import time
import requests

BASE_URL = "https://findmyclient.org/api"
API_TOKEN = "YOUR-API-TOKEN"


def search(query: str):
    # Start search job
    response = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/search",
        params={"query": query, "token": API_TOKEN},
        timeout=30
    )

    response.raise_for_status()
    data = response.json()

    job_id = data["job_id"]
    print(f"Job started: {job_id}")

    # Poll for results
    while True:
        result = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/result/{job_id}",
            timeout=30
        )

        result.raise_for_status()
        payload = result.json()
        status = payload["status"]

        if status == "completed":
            if payload.get("error"):
                raise Exception(payload["error"])

            return payload["result"]

        print("Still processing...")
        time.sleep(60)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    output = search("singapore cafe")
    print("\nFinal Result:\n")
    print(output)

docs.title: Start a Search Job
docs.text:
Start a new search job by sending a query to the API. The request is processed asynchronously and returns a job_id that can be used to retrieve results later.

bashpythonshell 
curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
  -H "token: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "singapore cafe"
  }'

import requests

response = requests.post(
    "https://findmyclient.org/api/search",
    headers={"token": "YOUR_API_TOKEN"},
    json={"query": "singapore cafe"}
)

response.json()

Invoke-RestMethod -Method POST `
  -Uri "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" `
  -Headers @{token="YOUR_API_TOKEN"} `
  -Body (@{query="singapore cafe"} | ConvertTo-Json)

docs.title: Get Results
docs.text:
Retrieve the status and results of a previously submitted search job using its job_id. Once processing is complete, the API returns structured company and contact data in a machine-readable JSON format.

bashpythonshell 
curl -X GET "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/YOUR_JOB_ID"

import requests

response = requests.get(
    "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/YOUR_JOB_ID",
)

response.json()

Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET `
  -Uri "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/YOUR_JOB_ID"

Tips

Search jobs are processed asynchronously. Save the returned job_id and poll the result endpoint until the status becomes completed.

docs.title: v0.0.1 - 13 May 2026
docs.text: Initial release improvements introducing rate limiting, validation, and security hardening for the FindMyClient API.

docs.title: Added Rate Limiting
docs.text:
• Introduced request rate limiting to protect the API from excessive usage
  • Implemented a global handler for HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
  • Returns a structured JSON response when rate limits are exceeded

docs.title: Search Request Validation
docs.text: A centralized validation layer was introduced for all search requests.

docs.title: Supported Input Sources
docs.text:
• JSON request body
  • Form data
  • Query parameters

docs.title: Validation Rules
docs.text:
• Query must be a string
  • Minimum length: 3 characters
  • Maximum length: 50 characters
  • Maximum word count: 8 words

docs.title: Security Improvements
docs.text:
Multiple safeguards were added to improve request safety and prevent abuse:

  • Blocks URL-based inputs (e.g. http://, www.)
  • Prevents common injection patterns (SQL-like and script-based inputs)
  • Detects repeated-character spam patterns
  • Rejects inputs with excessive non-alphanumeric content ratio

docs.title: Content Filtering
docs.text:
Keyword-based filtering was introduced to block unsafe or irrelevant queries:

  • Fraud-related terms
  • Credential/password-related terms
  • Gambling-related terms
  • Adult content-related terms

docs.title: Input Normalization
docs.text:
All incoming queries are normalized before processing:

  • Trims leading and trailing whitespace
  • Collapses multiple spaces into a single space
  • Standardizes query formatting for consistent processing

docs.title: Schema Improvements (Machine Readable Results)
docs.text:
Improved API response consistency for AI agents, automation systems, and developer integrations.

  • Standardized response structures across all endpoints
  • Improved machine-readable JSON formatting
  • Added explicit typing for key fields such as queries, metadata, and results
  • Ensured predictable JSON keys for agents and integrations
  • Added structured error objects with consistent error codes and messages

docs.title: API Usage Enforcement (Anti-Abuse Layer)
docs.text:
Strengthened API protection and abuse prevention systems.

  • Added stricter request validation at the gateway layer
  • Improved distributed rate-limit enforcement consistency
  • Added early request rejection for invalid or abusive traffic
  • Reduced unnecessary backend processing load from malformed requests

docs.title: Dashboard Improvements (Minor Fixes)
docs.text:
Several dashboard stability and UI improvements were introduced.

  • Fixed UI inconsistencies in dashboard data rendering
  • Improved spacing and alignment in metric panels
  • Corrected display issues affecting recent request logs
  • Enhanced stability of the real-time activity feed under high request volume

docs.title: Performance & Reliability Improvements
docs.text:
Significant improvements focused on system stability, request success rates, and overall production performance across Find My Client.

  • Introduced a validated proxy pool system with automatic scoring, rotation, and failure tracking to improve request reliability
  • Improved fallback handling to ensure consistent results when primary data sources fail
  • Enhanced email discovery reliability through more resilient routing and execution paths
  • Reduced failure rates under concurrent load and improved request success consistency
  • Strengthened production stability for long-running and high-volume operations

docs.title: Observability & System Visibility
docs.text:
Improved visibility into system behavior for better monitoring, debugging, and operational control.

  • Standardized logging structure across core services for consistent output formatting
  • Improved debug signal clarity for production troubleshooting
  • Enhanced traceability of request execution paths and failure states

docs.title: Data Integrity & Validation
docs.text:
Improved internal data handling to ensure more reliable and predictable outputs.

  • Strengthened structured data validation using typed models for consistency across processing layers
  • Improved handling of malformed or incomplete data responses
  • Increased overall reliability of downstream processing pipelines

docs.title: Platform Stability Improvements
docs.text:
Focused on reducing instability in production environments and improving execution consistency.

  • Improved concurrency handling under load to reduce race conditions and failures
  • Enhanced CI/CD execution control to ensure cleaner, version-controlled deployments
  • Improved containerized deployment stability and runtime consistency
  • Reduced request timeouts and improved long-running process reliability
